description: Gilfoyle dialect translation corpus. Business/verbose English → Gilfoyle encoded form. Used as the encoder rule source and as a standalone style guide for direct technical writing.

entries.business_english: might cause a race condition
entries.note: tilde prefix on the uncertain outcome; drop modal and causal verb

entries.business_english: may result in intermittent failures
entries.note: drop 'may result in', prefix outcome with ~

entries.business_english: likely caused by missing error handling
this is probably related to the cache TTL
entries.note: strip 'this is probably related to the'

entries.business_english: this might be a timing issue
this could indicate a memory leak
this appears to be caused by a deadlock
the issue seems to be in the auth middleware
entries.note: 'the issue seems to be in' → ~ + abbreviated target

entries.business_english: you may want to increase the timeout
you might want to consider using a mutex
entries.note: full hedge theater stripped; imperative remains

entries.business_english: the pool is exhausted because each request opens a new connection
entries.gilfoyle: Each req opens new conn → pool exhausted

entries.business_english: the token is invalid since the clock skew exceeds five minutes
the cache is stale; therefore users see outdated data
entries.gilfoyle: Cache stale → users see outdated data

entries.business_english: calling the API in a tight loop results in rate limiting
not releasing the connection leads to pool exhaustion
the request fails due to an expired certificate
the connection is closed by the server after thirty seconds
user input is interpolated directly into the query string
entries.gilfoyle: Interpolates user input directly into query string
entries.note: agent implicit (the code); copula deleted; active form

entries.business_english: errors are swallowed silently when promises are not chained correctly
entries.gilfoyle: Errors swallowed silently when promises not chained correctly

entries.business_english: avoid passive voice unless the agent is clearly irrelevant
entries.gilfoyle: Avoid passive voice unless agent clearly irrelevant
entries.note: directive for LLM: strip copula in passive when agent is irrelevant to the narrative

entries.business_english: mistakes were made during the deployment
entries.note: assign agent; postmortem passive deliberately obscures accountability — name the agent

entries.business_english: an unexpected degradation was experienced by users
an exception is thrown when the value is null
entries.note: runtime agent obvious; copula deleted; abbreviations

entries.business_english: I'd be happy to explain how this works
note that this behavior is expected
keep in mind that the default timeout is thirty seconds
entries.note: strip 'keep in mind that'; numeric normalised

entries.business_english: let me walk you through the solution
it is worth noting that this only affects production
please note that you will need to restart the server
the connection pool is being exhausted
the token is missing
the authentication flow is broken
the error is occurring on every request
the server is sending requests in parallel
the cache entries are stale
the event loop is blocked by synchronous parsing
the JWT token is expired
in order to ensure connections are always released
to make sure the token is set correctly
restart the server so that the changes take effect
even when an error occurs
in order to prevent unbounded growth
in order to avoid a race condition
you must use parameterized queries
you cannot use the wildcard origin in production
entries.gilfoyle: Cannot use wildcard origin in prod

entries.business_english: it is required that you configure the timeout
you should use a mutex to protect shared state
entries.gilfoyle: Use mutex to protect shared state

entries.business_english: we experienced an unexpected degradation in service quality
this issue may have impacted a subset of users
we will need to take the service offline
we are not able to confirm the root cause at this time
entries.note: strip corporate hedge; state the fact directly

entries.business_english: we have identified several learnings from this incident
entries.note: 'learnings' is not a word; 'identified' → implied by colon

entries.business_english: mistakes were made during the rollout
entries.note: name the agent; postmortem passive is accountability avoidance

entries.business_english: the regression was not caught by the test suite
a contributing factor was the lack of monitoring
the incident was resolved after approximately forty-five minutes
The following action items have been identified: add monitoring for pool exhaustion, configure alerts for connection counts, update the runbook with pool sizing guidelines.
entries.gilfoyle:
- [ ] Add monitoring for pool exhaustion
- [ ] Configure alerts for conn counts
- [ ] Update runbook with pool sizing guidelines
entries.note: parallel action items → GFM task list

entries.business_english: in the event that an exception is thrown
entries.note: 'in the event that' → 'if'; copula deletion removes 'is'

entries.business_english: due to the fact that each request opens a new connection
entries.gilfoyle: because each req opens new conn
entries.note: 'due to the fact that' → 'because'; then causation restructuring fires

entries.business_english: as a result of this, the pool is exhausted
entries.note: 'as a result of this' → '→'; copula deleted

entries.business_english: with respect to the remediation of this issue
it is the case that the token is missing
entries.note: 'it is the case that' → strip; copula deleted

entries.business_english: it is important to note that the configuration must be updated
entries.note: 'it is important to note that' → strip; 'you must' → 'Must'

entries.business_english: at this point in time, the root cause is unknown
entries.note: 'at this point in time' → strip; copula deleted

entries.business_english: for the purpose of ensuring connections are released
this gives rise to a cascading failure
this will be addressed in the near future
the exhaustion of the connection pool
entries.note: 'exhaustion of the X' → 'X exhaustion' (nominalised form + known tech noun)

entries.business_english: the configuration of the server
entries.note: 'configuration of the X' → 'X config' via genitive inversion + abbreviation

entries.business_english: the behavior of the component
the failure of the team
entries.note: 'team' not in tech noun list → NO inversion (avoids 'team failure' semantic drift)

entries.business_english: the configuration of the timeout
entries.note: 'the configuration of X' → 'configuring X'; then abbreviation fires

entries.business_english: the exhaustion of the pool
the rejection of the request
entries.note: 'the rejection of X' → 'rejecting X'; abbreviation applied

entries.business_english: the implementation of a circuit breaker
all of the available connections were consumed
each of the incoming requests was opening a new connection
entries.gilfoyle: each incoming req opens new conn

entries.business_english: the majority of users experienced degradation
a number of requests were failing
a subset of users may have received outdated data
the entirety of available connections were consumed
the service makes use of a synchronous pattern
this has an impact on overall latency
entries.note: 'has an impact on' → 'affects' or '→'; causal restructuring

entries.business_english: the scheduler will carry out an eviction of the pod
entries.note: 'carry out' → 'run'; genitive inversion: 'eviction of pod' → 'pod eviction'

entries.business_english: put in place a circuit breaker pattern
does not take into account the performance implications
entries.note: 'does not take into account' → 'ignores' (or 'does not account for')

entries.business_english: gave rise to a cascading failure
